title: ADK-102: Advanced Configs & Multi-model Setup
description: Dive deep into agent.yaml properties. Configure model backends, safety filters, context parameters, temperature, and tokens.

highlights.title: YAML Schema
highlights.description: Examine the nested schema fields of agent.yaml, including settings, safety filters, and execution properties.

highlights.title: Safety Thresholds
highlights.description: Set block limits for hate speech, harassment, sexually explicit content, and dangerous scenarios.

highlights.title: Backend Selection
highlights.description: Learn to alternate between models like Gemini Flash, Gemini Pro, and configure secondary fallbacks.

modules.title: 1. Exploring agent.yaml Directives and Schema Options

blocks.content: Exploring agent.yaml Directives

The `agent.yaml` file is the declarative manifest for any ADK agent. It dictates behavior parameters, safety thresholds, active tool registrations, and execution limits. Understanding the layout structure is vital for enterprise control.
YAML Schema Anatomy
A standard `agent.yaml` includes metadata headers, model directives, and parameter limits. Here is a configuration snippet highlighting these properties:

blocks.code:
metadata:
  name: "enterprise-assistant"
  version: "1.2.0"
settings:
  model: "gemini-1.5-pro"
  temperature: 0.2
  max_tokens: 4096
  concurrency_limit: 5

blocks.title: Configuration Validation
blocks.content: ADK validates the YAML properties against a strict JSON Schema at runtime. Invalid or unrecognized top-level keys will raise a validation error.
Check Your Understanding

Question 1: Which of the following defines the operational bounds like concurrency and temperature in ADK?
- A) The metadata section
- B) The settings block
- C) The tool definition list

Feedback: Option B is correct. Settings dictate execution parameters.

modules.title: 2. Configuring Safety Filters and Block Thresholds

blocks.content: Configuring Safety Filters
Enterprise agents must operate within ethical and security guidelines. ADK safety directives map directly to LLM content moderation categories, allowing you to reject malicious outputs.
Safety Block Configuration

You specify safety thresholds for four categories: Harassment, Hate Speech, Sexually Explicit, and Dangerous Content. Standard threshold parameters are `BLOCK_NONE`, `BLOCK_LOW_AND_ABOVE`, `BLOCK_MEDIUM_AND_ABOVE`, and `BLOCK_HIGH_AND_ABOVE`.

blocks.code:
safety_settings:
  - category: "HATE_SPEECH"
    threshold: "BLOCK_LOW_AND_ABOVE"
  - category: "HARASSMENT"
    threshold: "BLOCK_MEDIUM_AND_ABOVE"
  - category: "DANGEROUS_CONTENT"
    threshold: "BLOCK_LOW_AND_ABOVE"

blocks.title: Over-blocking Risks
blocks.content: Setting thresholds to BLOCK_LOW_AND_ABOVE might cause false positives, blocking benign queries that mention industry jargon. Balance security and usability.
Check Your Understanding

Question 1: What category controls queries suggesting self-harm or unsafe physical acts?
- A) HARASSMENT
- B) DANGEROUS_CONTENT
- C) HATE_SPEECH

Feedback: Option B is correct. DANGEROUS_CONTENT includes risk-prone physical actions.

modules.title: 3. Temperature, Top-k, Top-p, and Max Tokens

blocks.content: Model Tuning Parameters
Hyperparameters dictate the diversity and randomness of the model's text generation loops. Small adjustments drastically shift response formats.

items.title: Temperature
items.content: Controls response randomness. 0.0 is deterministic; 1.0 is creative.

items.title: Top-P
items.content: Nucleus sampling threshold, selecting tokens from a cumulative probability pool.

items.title: Top-K
items.content: Selects next token from top K most probable options.

blocks.content: Tuning YAML Setup

blocks.code:
settings:
  model: "gemini-1.5-flash"
  temperature: 0.1
  top_p: 0.95
  top_k: 40
  max_output_tokens: 1024

blocks.content: Check Your Understanding

Question 1: For code generation, what is the recommended temperature range?
- A) High (0.8 - 1.0)
- B) Low (0.0 - 0.2)
- C) Variable (0.5 - 0.7)

Feedback: Option B is correct. Code requires precise syntax, making low temperature settings optimal.
Multi-Model Backends
ADK allows defining secondary fallback models. If the primary model encounters rate limits (HTTP 429) or latency anomalies, the runner shifts tasks to the secondary provider.
Fallback Directives

blocks.title: Key Provisioning
blocks.content: Fallback models require matching API keys in the environment variables (e.g. GEMINI_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) to authorize requests dynamically.
Check Your Understanding

Question 1: What happens if the primary model throws a quota exceeded error and no fallbacks are configured?
- A) The runner sleeps indefinitely.
- B) The agent execution terminates immediately with a runtime error.
- C) The agent uses local static fallback templates.

Feedback: Option B is correct. Without fallback routing, standard exceptions fail the execution.
Lab: Multi-Model Configuration Switcher
In this lab, you will build a Python program that dynamically generates `agent.yaml` files depending on environment variables (e.g., swapping to flash for testing, pro for production).
Step 1: Write the Python Switcher
Step 2: Run the switcher and verify

blocks.code:
export AGENT_ENV=production
python switcher.py
cat agent.yaml

blocks.content: Check Your Understanding

Question 1: Why is separating configurations dynamically useful in CI/CD chains?
- A) It speeds up code compiler loops.
- B) It allows testing with low-cost model backends in staging and high-accuracy models in prod.
- C) It removes the need for OAuth credentials.

Feedback: Option B is correct. Dynamic configurations save costs and optimize testing.
